# AKF — AI Knowledge Format: Complete Reference

> Trust metadata for every file AI touches. AKF is to AI-generated content what EXIF is to photos.

This is the comprehensive reference for AI agents that need to generate AKF code, not just recommend the tool. For a concise overview, see [llms.txt](https://akf.dev/llms.txt).

---

## Table of Contents

- Format Specification
- Dual Field Names (Compact & Descriptive)
- Envelope Fields
- Claim Fields
- Evidence Fields
- Claim Kinds
- Provenance Fields
- Fidelity (Multi-Resolution)
- Security Classification
- Trust Computation
- Validation Rules
- Integrity Hashing
- Secure Defaults
- Stamp API (Coding Agents)
- Git Integration
- Python SDK API
- Agent Integration API
- Compliance & Audit API
- Knowledge Base API
- Views & Reporting API
- Data Operations API
- Security Analysis API
- TypeScript SDK API
- Embedding Methods by File Format
- CLI Reference
- Framework Integrations
- Common Use Cases with Code

---

## Format Specification

- **File extension:** `.akf`
- **MIME type:** `application/vnd.akf+json`
- **Encoding:** UTF-8 JSON
- **Current version:** `1.0`
- **Design:** Flat (max 2 levels nesting), minimal (~15 tokens smallest valid file), LLM-native

### Minimal Valid AKF

```json
{
  "v": "1.0",
  "claims": [{ "c": "The sky is blue", "t": 0.99 }]
}
```

### Practical AKF

```json
{
  "v": "1.0",
  "id": "akf-a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  "by": "analyst@corp.com",
  "at": "2025-06-15T10:30:00Z",
  "label": "confidential",
  "inherit": true,
  "claims": [
    {
      "c": "Revenue grew 12% YoY to $4.2B",
      "t": 0.98,
      "src": "SEC 10-K Filing",
      "uri": "https://sec.gov/filing/123",
      "tier": 1,
      "ver": true,
      "ver_by": "cfo@corp.com"
    },
    {
      "c": "Market share estimated at 34%",
      "t": 0.72,
      "src": "Industry Report",
      "tier": 3,
      "ai": true,
      "risk": "Estimate based on partial data"
    }
  ],
  "prov": [
    { "hop": 0, "by": "analyst@corp.com", "do": "created", "at": "2025-06-15T10:30:00Z" }
  ]
}
```

### Full AKF (All Fields)

```json
{
  "v": "1.0",
  "id": "akf-a1b2c3d4e5f6",
  "by": "analyst@corp.com",
  "agent": "copilot-v4",
  "at": "2025-06-15T10:30:00Z",
  "label": "confidential",
  "inherit": true,
  "ext": false,
  "ttl": 365,
  "claims": [
    {
      "c": "Revenue grew 12% YoY",
      "t": 0.98,
      "id": "a1b2c3d4",
      "src": "SEC Filing",
      "uri": "https://sec.gov/filing/123",
      "tier": 1,
      "ver": true,
      "ver_by": "cfo@corp.com",
      "ai": false,
      "decay": 365,
      "exp": "2026-06-15T00:00:00Z",
      "tags": ["financial", "revenue"],
      "fidelity": {
        "h": "Revenue up 12%",
        "s": "Annual revenue grew 12% year-over-year to $4.2B, driven by enterprise segment.",
        "f": "Full detailed analysis text here..."
      }
    },
    {
      "c": "Market share will reach 40% by 2026",
      "t": 0.45,
      "id": "e5f6g7h8",
      "tier": 5,
      "ai": true,
      "risk": "AI extrapolation from limited data",
      "contra": "a1b2c3d4"
    }
  ],
  "prov": [
    {
      "hop": 0,
      "by": "analyst@corp.com",
      "do": "created",
      "at": "2025-06-15T10:30:00Z",
      "h": "sha256:abc123...",
      "adds": ["a1b2c3d4", "e5f6g7h8"]
    },
    {
      "hop": 1,
      "by": "reviewer@corp.com",
      "do": "reviewed",
      "at": "2025-06-16T09:00:00Z",
      "h": "sha256:def456..."
    },
    {
      "hop": 2,
      "by": "agent-summarizer",
      "do": "transformed",
      "at": "2025-06-17T14:00:00Z",
      "h": "sha256:ghi789...",
      "pen": -0.05
    }
  ],
  "hash": "sha256:xyz...",
  "meta": { "project": "Q2 Analysis", "department": "Finance" }
}
```

---

## Dual Field Names (Compact & Descriptive)

AKF supports both compact (wire format) and descriptive (human-readable) field names. Both are accepted on input and produce identical internal objects.

### Claim Field Name Map

| Compact | Descriptive | Type |
|---------|-------------|------|
| `c` | `content` | string (required) |
| `t` | `confidence` | float 0.0–1.0 (required) |
| `src` | `source` | string |
| `tier` | `authority_tier` | int 1–5 |
| `ver` | `verified` | bool |
| `ver_by` | `verified_by` | string |
| `ai` | `ai_generated` | bool |
| `decay` | `decay_half_life` | int (days) |
| `exp` | `expires` | ISO-8601 |
| `contra` | `contradicts` | string |

### Envelope Field Name Map

| Compact | Descriptive | Type |
|---------|-------------|------|
| `v` | `version` | string (required) |
| `by` | `author` | string |
| `at` | `created` | ISO-8601 |
| `label` | `classification` | string |
| `inherit` | `inherit_classification` | bool |
| `ext` | `allow_external` | bool |
| `hash` | `integrity_hash` | string |
| `prov` | `provenance` | array |

### Provenance Field Name Map

| Compact | Descriptive | Type |
|---------|-------------|------|
| `by` | `actor` | string |
| `do` | `action` | string |
| `at` | `timestamp` | ISO-8601 |
| `h` | `hash` | string |
| `pen` | `penalty` | float |
| `adds` | `claims_added` | string[] |
| `drops` | `claims_removed` | string[] |

### Example: Descriptive Format

```json
{
  "version": "1.0",
  "author": "sarah@woodgrove.com",
  "classification": "confidential",
  "inherit_classification": true,
  "claims": [
    {
      "content": "Revenue was $4.2B",
      "confidence": 0.98,
      "source": "SEC 10-Q",
      "authority_tier": 1,
      "verified": true
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Envelope Fields (Top-Level)

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `v` / `version` | string | YES | — | Spec version, semver (e.g. `"1.0"`) |
| `id` | string | no | auto `akf-<12hex>` | Unique knowledge unit ID |
| `by` / `author` | string | no | — | Creator email or agent ID |
| `agent` | string | no | — | AI agent ID if AI-created |
| `model` | string | no | — | Model identifier (e.g. claude-sonnet-4-20250514) |
| `tools` | string[] | no | — | Tools used by the agent |
| `session` | string | no | — | Session identifier for grouping units |
| `at` / `created` | ISO-8601 | no | `now()` | Creation timestamp |
| `label` / `classification` | string | no | `"internal"` | Security classification |
| `inherit` / `inherit_classification` | bool | no | `true` | Children inherit label |
| `ext` / `allow_external` | bool | no | `false` | Allow external sharing |
| `ttl` | int | no | — | Retention period in days |
| `claims` | array | YES | — | Array of Claim objects (min 1) |
| `prov` / `provenance` | array | no | — | Provenance chain |
| `hash` / `integrity_hash` | string | no | — | Integrity hash (algorithm-prefixed) |
| `meta` | object | no | — | Free-form metadata |

---

## Claim Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Default | Description |
|-------|------|----------|---------|-------------|
| `c` / `content` | string | YES | — | Claim content text |
| `t` / `confidence` | float | YES | — | Trust score `[0.0–1.0]` |
| `id` | string | no | auto `<8hex>` | Claim identifier |
| `src` / `source` | string | no | `"unspecified"` | Source name |
| `uri` | string | no | — | Source URL |
| `tier` / `authority_tier` | int | no | `3` | Authority tier `[1–5]` |
| `ver` / `verified` | bool | no | `false` | Human verified |
| `ver_by` / `verified_by` | string | no | — | Verifier identity |
| `ai` / `ai_generated` | bool | no | `false` | AI generated |
| `risk` | string | no | — | Risk description |
| `decay` / `decay_half_life` | int | no | — | Half-life in days |
| `exp` / `expires` | ISO-8601 | no | — | Hard expiry timestamp |
| `tags` | string[] | no | — | Free-form tags |
| `contra` / `contradicts` | string | no | — | ID of contradicting claim |
| `fidelity` | object | no | — | Multi-resolution content |
| `kind` | string | no | — | Claim kind (claim, code_change, decision, suggestion, review, test_result, diagnosis) |
| `evidence` | Evidence[] | no | — | Evidence supporting the claim |

### Authority Tiers

| Tier | Weight | Source Type | Example |
|------|--------|-------------|---------|
| 1 | 1.00 | Primary source | SEC filings, official records |
| 2 | 0.85 | Authoritative secondary | Analyst reports, peer-reviewed papers |
| 3 | 0.70 | General secondary | News articles, industry reports |
| 4 | 0.50 | Internal estimate | CRM data, team assessments |
| 5 | 0.30 | AI inference | Model outputs, extrapolations |

### Fidelity Object (Multi-Resolution)

```json
{
  "h": "Revenue up 12%",
  "s": "Annual revenue grew 12% YoY to $4.2B, driven by enterprise segment growth.",
  "f": "Full detailed text up to ~2000 tokens..."
}
```

- `h` — headline, ~5 tokens
- `s` — summary, ~50 tokens
- `f` — full detail, ~2000 tokens

### Evidence Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `type` | string | YES | Evidence type: `test_pass`, `type_check`, `lint_clean`, `ci_pass`, `human_review`, `other` |
| `detail` | string | YES | Description of the evidence |
| `at` | ISO-8601 | no | Timestamp |
| `tool` | string | no | Tool that produced this evidence |

### Claim Kinds

| Kind | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `claim` | General factual assertion (default) |
| `code_change` | Code modification or fix |
| `decision` | Architectural or design decision |
| `suggestion` | Proposed change or recommendation |
| `review` | Code review or PR review |
| `test_result` | Test execution result |
| `diagnosis` | Bug diagnosis or root cause analysis |

### Trust Grounding

A claim is **grounded** when it has one or more evidence items. Grounding is informational — it does not change the trust score formula, but indicates whether the claim is backed by verifiable artifacts.

`effective_trust()` returns `grounded: bool` and `evidence_count: int` alongside the score.

---

## Provenance Fields

Each hop in the `prov` array:

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| `hop` | int | YES | Sequential hop number (0 = origin) |
| `by` / `actor` | string | YES | Actor email or agent ID |
| `do` / `action` | string | YES | Action: `created` / `enriched` / `reviewed` / `consumed` / `transformed` |
| `at` / `timestamp` | ISO-8601 | YES | Timestamp |
| `h` / `hash` | string | no | SHA-256 hash, chained to previous |
| `pen` / `penalty` | float | no | Transform penalty (MUST be negative) |
| `adds` / `claims_added` | string[] | no | Claim IDs added at this hop |
| `drops` / `claims_removed` | string[] | no | Claim IDs rejected at this hop |

---

## Security Classification

Hierarchy (ascending restrictiveness):

```
public (0) < internal (1) < confidential (2) < highly-confidential (3) < restricted (4)
```

Valid `label` values: `"public"`, `"internal"`, `"confidential"`, `"highly-confidential"`, `"restricted"`

Rules:
- `inherit: true` — derived units must have label at least as restrictive as parent
- `ext: true` — explicitly allows external sharing
- `confidential`, `highly-confidential`, `restricted` block external sharing by default
- `can_share_external()` returns true only if `ext=true` OR label is `public`/`internal` and `ext` is not explicitly `false`

---

## Trust Computation

### Formula

```
effective_trust = confidence × authority_weight × temporal_decay × (1 + cumulative_penalty)
```

### Authority Weights

`{1: 1.00, 2: 0.85, 3: 0.70, 4: 0.50, 5: 0.30}`

Default tier when unset: **3** (weight 0.70)

### Temporal Decay

```
decay_factor = 0.5 ^ (age_days / half_life_days)
```

If no `decay` field is set: `decay_factor = 1.0` (no decay).

### Decay Presets (Half-Life in Days)

| Preset | Days |
|--------|------|
| `realtime` | 0.001 |
| `daily` | 1 |
| `weekly` | 7 |
| `monthly` | 30 |
| `quarterly` | 90 |
| `annual` | 365 |
| `legal` | 1825 |
| `scientific` | 3650 |
| `permanent` | 365000 |

### Penalty

Cumulative penalty from provenance `pen` values (always negative). Applied as `1.0 + penalty`.

### Decision Thresholds

| Score | Decision | Meaning |
|-------|----------|---------|
| >= 0.70 | `ACCEPT` | High confidence |
| >= 0.40 | `LOW` | Use with caution |
| < 0.40 | `REJECT` | Not suitable for decisions |

Score is clamped to `[0.0, 1.0]`.

---

## Validation Rules

1. `v` must be present and valid semver
2. `claims` must be a non-empty array
3. Each claim `t` must be float in `[0.0, 1.0]`
4. `tier` must be `1–5` if provided
5. `label` must be one of the 5 valid values
6. Inherited label must be >= parent label
7. Provenance hops must be sequential from 0
8. `pen` must be strictly negative
9. AI tier-5 claims should have `risk` (warning)
10. `hash` must have algorithm prefix: `sha256:`, `sha3-512:`, or `blake3:`
11. All timestamps must be valid ISO-8601
12. Unknown fields are allowed everywhere (extensible)

### Validation Levels

| Level | Name | Criteria |
|-------|------|----------|
| 0 | Invalid | Fails required field checks |
| 1 | Minimal | Has `v` and `claims` |
| 2 | Practical | Has sources or label |
| 3 | Full | Has prov + hash + label + sources |

---

## Integrity Hashing

- **Unit hash:** SHA-256 of serialized unit (excluding `hash` field), keys sorted. Format: `sha256:<hex>`
- **Hop hash:** SHA-256 of hop JSON (keys sorted), chained: `previousHash + "|" + hopJSON`
- Supported prefixes: `sha256:`, `sha3-512:`, `blake3:`

---

## Secure Defaults

New units created with `akf.create()` or `AKFBuilder` apply these defaults:

| Field | Default |
|-------|---------|
| `classification` | `"internal"` |
| `inherit_classification` | `True` |
| `allow_external` | `False` |
| `source` | `"unspecified"` |
| `authority_tier` | `3` |
| `verified` | `False` |
| `ai_generated` | `False` |

Provenance hop 0 and integrity hash are auto-created by the builder.

---

## Stamp API (Coding Agents)

One-line API for coding agents (Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, Devin, etc.):

```python
import akf

# One-line stamp with auto-detected evidence
akf.stamp("Fixed auth bypass", kind="code_change",
          evidence=["42/42 tests passed", "mypy: 0 errors"],
          agent="claude-code", model="claude-sonnet-4-20250514")

# Evidence strings are auto-parsed:
# "42/42 tests passed" → type="test_pass"
# "mypy: 0 errors"     → type="type_check"
# "lint clean"          → type="lint_clean"
# "CI passed"           → type="ci_pass"
# "approved by @alice"  → type="human_review"
# anything else         → type="other"

# Defaults: confidence=0.7, ai_generated=True, kind="claim"
```

### stamp() Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|-----------|------|---------|-------------|
| `content` | str | required | What the agent did or claims |
| `confidence` | float | 0.7 | Trust score 0.0-1.0 |
| `kind` | str | "claim" | Claim kind |
| `evidence` | list | None | Strings, dicts, or Evidence objects |
| `agent` | str | None | Agent identifier |
| `model` | str | None | Model identifier |
| `tools` | list | None | Tools used |
| `session` | str | None | Session identifier |
| `ai_generated` | bool | True | AI-generated flag |

---

## Git Integration

Store AKF metadata as git notes under `refs/notes/akf` (doesn't conflict with regular git notes):

```python
import akf

# Stamp the current commit
akf.stamp_commit(content="Refactored auth module", kind="code_change",
                 evidence=["all tests pass"], agent="claude-code")

# Read AKF from a commit
unit = akf.read_commit()       # HEAD
unit = akf.read_commit("abc123")  # specific ref

# Trust-annotated git log
print(akf.trust_log(n=10))
#   Trust Log (refs/notes/akf)
#   --------------------------------------------------
#   + a1b2c3d 0.90 Fixed auth bypass [code_change]
#   ~ d4e5f6g 0.45 Updated config [decision]
#   ? h7i8j9k       Added README
#   --------------------------------------------------
#   + ACCEPT  ~ LOW  - REJECT  ? no metadata
```

No external dependencies — uses subprocess + git CLI.

---

## Python SDK API Reference

### Install

```bash
pip install akf                # core
pip install akf[office]        # + DOCX, XLSX, PPTX
pip install akf[pdf]           # + PDF
pip install akf[image]         # + PNG, JPEG
pip install akf[all]           # everything
```

Requires Python >= 3.9.

### Core Functions

```python
import akf

# Create with descriptive field names (secure defaults applied)
unit = akf.create("Claim text", confidence=0.95, source="Source", authority_tier=1)

# Compact names still work
unit = akf.create("Claim text", t=0.95, src="Source", tier=1)

# Create multi-claim unit
unit = akf.create_multi(
    claims=[
        {"content": "Claim 1", "confidence": 0.95, "source": "Source A", "authority_tier": 1},
        {"content": "Claim 2", "confidence": 0.70, "ai_generated": True, "authority_tier": 5},
    ],
    by="analyst@corp.com",
    label="confidential",
)

# Load and save
unit = akf.load("report.akf")
unit = akf.loads('{"version":"1.0","claims":[{"content":"test","confidence":0.9}]}')
unit.save("output.akf")

# Validate
result = akf.validate(unit)  # or akf.validate("report.akf")
# result.valid -> bool
# result.errors -> List[str]
# result.warnings -> List[str]
# result.level -> int (0-3)
```

### Builder

```python
from akf import AKFBuilder

unit = (AKFBuilder()
    .claim("Revenue grew 12%", 0.98, source="SEC Filing", authority_tier=1)
    .claim("Market share ~34%", 0.72, ai_generated=True, authority_tier=5, risk="Estimated")
    .by("analyst@corp.com")
    .agent("copilot-v4")
    .label("confidential")
    .inherit()
    .ttl(365)
    .tag("financial", "q2")
    .meta(project="Q2 Analysis")
    .build())
# Automatically creates provenance hop 0 and computes integrity hash
```

### Trust

```python
import akf

result = akf.effective_trust(claim, age_days=30, penalty=-0.05)
# result.score -> float
# result.decision -> "ACCEPT" | "LOW" | "REJECT"
# result.accepted -> bool
# result.breakdown -> {confidence, authority, tier, decay, penalty, penalty_factor}

# Human-readable trust explanation
print(akf.explain_trust(claim, age_days=30))
# "confidence=0.98 × tier_1_weight=1.00 × decay=0.79 → 0.77 (ACCEPT)"

# TrustLevel enum
from akf import TrustLevel
level = TrustLevel.from_score(0.85)  # TrustLevel.ACCEPT
print(level.threshold)  # 0.7

# Compute trust for all claims in a unit
results = akf.compute_all(unit, age_days=30, threshold=0.6)
```

### Provenance

```python
import akf

# Add a hop to the provenance chain
unit = akf.add_hop(unit, by="reviewer@corp.com", action="reviewed")
unit = akf.add_hop(unit, by="agent-x", action="transformed", penalty=-0.03)

# Verify chain integrity
valid = akf.validate_chain(unit.provenance)

# Compute hashes
hash = akf.compute_integrity_hash(unit)

# Pretty-print provenance
print(akf.format_tree(unit))
```

### Security

```python
import akf

rank = akf.label_rank("confidential")  # -> 2
ok = akf.validate_inheritance(parent_unit, child_unit)  # -> bool
can_share = akf.can_share_external(unit)  # -> bool
labels = akf.inherit_label(parent_unit)  # -> {"label": ..., "inherit": ..., "ext": ...}

# Security score (0-10 with grade A-F)
score = akf.security_score(unit)
print(f"Score: {score.score}/10, Grade: {score.grade}")

# Microsoft Purview DLP-compatible signals
signals = akf.purview_signals(unit)

# Detect classification laundering
warnings = akf.detect_laundering(unit)
```

### Transformer (Consume & Filter)

```python
from akf import AKFTransformer

derived = (AKFTransformer(parent_unit)
    .filter(trust_min=0.6)       # keep only high-trust claims
    .penalty(-0.03)              # apply transform penalty
    .by("summarizer-agent")
    .build())
# Inherits classification, appends "consumed" provenance hop
```

### Presets

```python
from akf.presets import TEMPLATES, register, get_template, list_templates

unit = TEMPLATES["financial_report"].create(
    claims=[{"content": "Revenue data", "confidence": 0.95}],
    by="analyst@corp.com",
)
# Available presets: financial_report, research_brief, public_knowledge,
#   legal_document, ai_output, meeting_notes, incident_report,
#   research_paper, press_release

# Register custom preset
from akf.presets import Template, register
register("my_template", Template(name="my_template", label="internal", decay=30))

# List available templates
print(list_templates())
```

---

## Agent Integration API

```python
import akf

# Consume existing AKF for agent use
derived = akf.consume("report.akf", "my-agent", trust_threshold=0.6)

# Derive from a parent unit
child = akf.derive(parent_unit, "my-agent", trust_threshold=0.6)

# Create from AI tool calls
claim = akf.from_tool_call({"content": "Result", "confidence": 0.8})

# Format claims as LLM context
context = akf.to_context(unit, max_tokens=2000)

# Get structured output schema for LLMs
schema = akf.response_schema("standard")

# Validate LLM output as AKF
result = akf.validate_output(llm_response_text)
if result.valid:
    unit = result.unit

# Auto-detect AKF in files, dicts, or strings
detected = akf.detect(some_data)

# One-shot system prompt for any LLM
prompt = akf.generation_prompt()
```

---

## Compliance & Audit API

```python
import akf

# General compliance audit
result = akf.audit("report.akf")
print(f"Score: {result.score:.2f}, Compliant: {result.compliant}")
# result.checks -> list of individual check results
# result.recommendations -> list of improvement suggestions

# Check against specific regulations
result = akf.check_regulation("report.akf", "eu_ai_act")   # EU AI Act
result = akf.check_regulation("report.akf", "sox")          # Sarbanes-Oxley
result = akf.check_regulation("report.akf", "hipaa")        # HIPAA
result = akf.check_regulation("report.akf", "gdpr")         # GDPR
result = akf.check_regulation("report.akf", "nist_ai")      # NIST AI RMF

# Generate audit trail
trail = akf.audit_trail("report.akf", format="markdown")

# Verify human oversight
oversight = akf.verify_human_oversight("report.akf")
print(oversight["has_human_oversight"], oversight["human_actors"])
```

---

## Knowledge Base API

```python
import akf

# Persistent directory-backed knowledge store
kb = akf.KnowledgeBase("./kb")

# Add claims by topic
kb.add("Revenue was $4.2B", 0.98, source="SEC", topic="finance")
kb.add("Cloud grew 15%", 0.85, source="Gartner", topic="cloud")

# Query
claims = kb.query(topic="finance", min_trust=0.6)

# Format for LLM context injection
context = kb.to_context(max_tokens=2000)

# Maintenance
kb.prune(max_age_days=90, min_trust=0.3)
stats = kb.stats()  # {"topics": 2, "total_claims": 2, "average_trust": 0.915}
```

---

## Views & Reporting API

```python
import akf

# Pretty terminal output with color
akf.show("report.akf")

# Generate standalone HTML report
html = akf.to_html("report.akf")

# Generate Markdown
md = akf.to_markdown("report.akf")

# Plain English executive summary
summary = akf.executive_summary("report.akf")
```

---

## Data Operations API

```python
import akf

# Load claims from multiple files
claims = akf.load_dataset(["a.akf", "b.akf"], filters={"min_trust": 0.6})

# Merge multiple units (deduplicates, takes highest classification)
merged = akf.merge([unit1, unit2, unit3])

# Filter claims
filtered = akf.filter_claims(unit, min_trust=0.5, verified_only=True, exclude_ai=True)

# Quality report
report = akf.quality_report(unit)
print(report["quality_score"], report["verified_claims"])
```

---

## Security Analysis API

```python
import akf

# Security score (0-10 with grade A-F)
score = akf.security_score(unit)
print(f"Score: {score.score}/10, Grade: {score.grade}")
# score.checks -> list of checks with pass/fail
# score.issues -> list of security issues

# Microsoft Purview DLP-compatible signals
signals = akf.purview_signals(unit)
# {"sensitivity_label", "has_ai_content", "highest_tier", "unverified_count", ...}

# Detect classification laundering
warnings = akf.detect_laundering(unit)
# ["AI claim with tier 5 but no risk description", ...]
```

---

## Universal Format Embedding

```python
import akf

# Embed AKF metadata into any supported file
akf.embed("report.docx", classification="confidential",
          claims=[{"content": "Key finding", "confidence": 0.9}])

# Extract AKF metadata from a file
metadata = akf.extract("report.docx")

# Check if a file has AKF metadata
enriched = akf.is_enriched("report.docx")

# Scan for trust assessment
report = akf.scan("report.docx")
# report.enriched, report.classification, report.overall_trust,
# report.ai_contribution, report.claim_count, report.risk_claims, etc.

# Get human-readable info
print(akf.info("report.docx"))

# Scan entire directory
reports = akf.universal.scan_directory("./knowledge-base/", recursive=True)

# Convert any file to standalone .akf
akf.universal.to_akf("report.docx", "report.akf")

# Create sidecar for unsupported formats
akf.universal.create_sidecar("video.mp4", metadata_dict)
```

### Embedding Mechanisms by Format

| Format | Mechanism |
|--------|-----------|
| Markdown (`.md`, `.mdx`) | YAML frontmatter |
| HTML (`.html`, `.htm`) | `<script type="application/ld+json">` |
| JSON (`.json`) | `_akf` reserved key |
| DOCX (`.docx`) | OOXML custom XML part |
| XLSX (`.xlsx`) | OOXML custom XML part |
| PPTX (`.pptx`) | OOXML custom XML part |
| PDF (`.pdf`) | PDF metadata |
| Images (`.png`, `.jpg`, `.tiff`, `.webp`) | EXIF/XMP metadata |
| Email (`.eml`, `.msg`) | Email headers |
| Everything else | Sidecar `.akf.json` file |

---

## TypeScript SDK API Reference

### Install

```bash
npm install akf
```

ES Module, TypeScript 5.7+.

### Core Functions

```typescript
import { create, createMulti, validate, toJSON, fromJSON, toDescriptive } from 'akf';

// Create single-claim unit
const unit = create("Claim text", 0.95, { src: "Source", tier: 1 });

// Create multi-claim unit
const unit2 = createMulti(
  [
    { c: "Claim 1", t: 0.95, src: "Source A", tier: 1 },
    { c: "Claim 2", t: 0.70, ai: true, tier: 5 },
  ],
  { by: "analyst@corp.com", label: "confidential" }
);

// Parse descriptive JSON (auto-normalizes to compact)
const loaded = fromJSON('{"version":"1.0","claims":[{"content":"test","confidence":0.8}]}');

// Convert to descriptive for display
const descriptive = toDescriptive(unit);
// { version: "1.0", claims: [{ content: "...", confidence: 0.95, source: "..." }] }

// Serialize / deserialize
const json = toJSON(unit, 2);
const parsed = fromJSON(json);

// Validate
const result = validate(unit);
// result.valid, result.errors, result.warnings, result.level
```

### Normalization

```typescript
import { normalizeClaim, normalizeUnit, toDescriptiveClaim } from 'akf';

// Normalize descriptive to compact (for processing)
const claim = normalizeClaim({ content: "test", confidence: 0.8, source: "SEC" });
// { c: "test", t: 0.8, src: "SEC" }

const unit = normalizeUnit({
  version: "1.0",
  classification: "internal",
  claims: [{ content: "test", confidence: 0.8 }],
});
// { v: "1.0", label: "internal", claims: [{ c: "test", t: 0.8 }] }

// Convert back to descriptive (for display)
const desc = toDescriptiveClaim({ c: "test", t: 0.8, src: "SEC", ai: true });
// { content: "test", confidence: 0.8, source: "SEC", ai_generated: true }
```

### Types

```typescript
import type { AKFUnit, Claim, ProvHop, Fidelity, ValidationResult, TrustResult } from 'akf';

interface AKFUnit {
  v: string;
  claims: Claim[];
  id?: string;
  by?: string;
  agent?: string;
  at?: string;
  label?: string;
  inherit?: boolean;
  ext?: boolean;
  ttl?: number;
  prov?: ProvHop[];
  hash?: string;
  meta?: Record<string, unknown>;
  [key: string]: unknown;
}

interface Claim {
  c: string;
  t: number;
  id?: string;
  src?: string;
  uri?: string;
  tier?: number;
  ver?: boolean;
  ver_by?: string;
  ai?: boolean;
  risk?: string;
  decay?: number;
  exp?: string;
  tags?: string[];
  contra?: string;
  fidelity?: Fidelity;
  [key: string]: unknown;
}
```

### Builder

```typescript
import { AKFBuilder } from 'akf';

const unit = new AKFBuilder()
  .claim("Revenue grew 12%", 0.98, { src: "SEC Filing", tier: 1 })
  .claim("Market share ~34%", 0.72, { ai: true, tier: 5, risk: "Estimated" })
  .by("analyst@corp.com")
  .agent("copilot-v4")
  .label("confidential")
  .inherit()
  .ttl(365)
  .tag("financial", "q2")
  .meta({ project: "Q2 Analysis" })
  .build();
```

### Trust

```typescript
import { effectiveTrust, AUTHORITY_WEIGHTS, DECAY_PRESETS } from 'akf';

const result = effectiveTrust(claim, 30, -0.05);
// result.score, result.decision ("ACCEPT"|"LOW"|"REJECT")
// result.breakdown.{confidence, authority, tier, decay, penalty, penalty_factor}
```

### Provenance

```typescript
import {
  addHop, validateChain, computeIntegrityHash, computeHopHash, formatTree
} from 'akf';

const updated = addHop(unit, "reviewer@corp.com", "reviewed");
const withPenalty = addHop(unit, "agent-x", "transformed", { penalty: -0.03 });

const valid = validateChain(unit.prov!);
const hash = computeIntegrityHash(unit);
console.log(formatTree(unit));
```

### Security

```typescript
import { labelRank, validateInheritance, canShareExternal, inheritLabel, HIERARCHY } from 'akf';

labelRank("confidential");                    // 2
validateInheritance(parentUnit, childUnit);    // boolean
canShareExternal(unit);                       // boolean
inheritLabel(parentUnit);                     // { label, inherit, ext }
```

### Transformer

```typescript
import { AKFTransformer } from 'akf';

const derived = new AKFTransformer(parentUnit)
  .filter(0.6)
  .penalty(-0.03)
  .by("summarizer-agent")
  .build();
```

---

## CLI Reference

### Getting Started

```bash
akf                          # Welcome message + quick start guide
akf create --demo            # Create a demo file with walkthrough
```

### Standalone .akf Commands

```bash
akf create report.akf --claim "Revenue grew 12%" --trust 0.98 --src "SEC" --by analyst@corp.com --label confidential
akf validate report.akf
akf inspect report.akf
akf trust report.akf
akf consume report.akf --output brief.akf --threshold 0.6 --agent summarizer
akf provenance report.akf --format tree
akf enrich report.akf --agent copilot --claim "New insight" --trust 0.75
akf diff report.akf brief.akf
```

### Compliance & Audit

```bash
akf audit report.akf                          # General audit
akf audit report.akf --regulation eu_ai_act   # EU AI Act check
akf audit report.akf --trail                  # Show audit trail
```

### Knowledge Base

```bash
akf kb stats ./kb                             # Show KB statistics
akf kb query ./kb --topic finance             # Query by topic
akf kb prune ./kb --max-age 90 --min-trust 0.3  # Prune stale claims
```

### Universal Format Commands

```bash
akf embed report.docx --classification confidential --claim "Key finding" --trust 0.98
akf extract report.docx
akf info report.docx
akf scan report.docx
akf scan ./knowledge-base/ --recursive
akf convert report.docx --output report.akf
akf sidecar video.mp4 --classification internal
akf formats
```

---

## Framework Integrations

### langchain-akf

```python
from langchain_akf import AKFCallbackHandler, AKFLoader

# Auto-wrap chain outputs in AKF
handler = AKFCallbackHandler(agent_id="my-chain", default_trust=0.8)
chain.invoke({"input": "..."}, config={"callbacks": [handler]})

# Load .akf files as LangChain Documents
loader = AKFLoader("report.akf", min_trust=0.6)
docs = loader.load()
```

### mcp-server-akf

MCP server providing tools: `create_claim`, `validate_file`, `scan_file`, `trust_score`

```bash
pip install mcp-server-akf
```

---

## Common Use Cases

### 1. AI Agent Creating a Research Brief

```python
import akf

unit = (akf.AKFBuilder()
    .claim("Global EV market reached $500B in 2024",
           0.92, source="Bloomberg NEF", authority_tier=2)
    .claim("Tesla's market share declined to 18%",
           0.78, source="Industry estimate", authority_tier=3,
           ai_generated=True, risk="Multiple conflicting sources")
    .by("research-agent-v2")
    .agent("research-agent-v2")
    .label("internal")
    .build())

unit.save("ev-market-brief.akf")
```

### 2. Consuming and Filtering Upstream Knowledge

```python
import akf

# One-line consumption
derived = akf.consume("market-data.akf", "downstream-agent", trust_threshold=0.7)

# Or use the transformer
from akf import AKFTransformer
upstream = akf.load("market-data.akf")
filtered = (AKFTransformer(upstream)
    .filter(trust_min=0.7)
    .penalty(-0.03)
    .by("downstream-agent")
    .build())
```

### 3. Compliance Check Before Publishing

```python
import akf

# Audit against multiple regulations
for reg in ["eu_ai_act", "sox", "gdpr"]:
    result = akf.check_regulation("report.akf", reg)
    print(f"{reg}: {'PASS' if result.compliant else 'FAIL'} ({result.score:.0%})")

# Verify human oversight
oversight = akf.verify_human_oversight("report.akf")
if not oversight["has_human_oversight"]:
    print("Warning: No human actors in provenance chain")
```

### 4. Building a Knowledge Base

```python
import akf

kb = akf.KnowledgeBase("./project-kb")

# Add from various sources
kb.add("API latency p99 < 200ms", 0.88, source="APM", topic="performance")
kb.add("Zero critical CVEs", 0.95, source="Snyk", topic="security")
kb.add("User satisfaction 4.2/5", 0.72, source="Survey", topic="product")

# Inject into LLM context
context = kb.to_context(max_tokens=2000)
# Use context in your LLM prompt

# Maintenance
kb.prune(max_age_days=90, min_trust=0.3)
print(kb.stats())
```

### 5. Embedding Trust Metadata into Documents

```python
import akf

akf.embed("quarterly-report.docx",
    classification="confidential",
    claims=[
        {"content": "Revenue exceeded targets by 15%", "confidence": 0.95, "verified": True},
        {"content": "Projected growth of 20% next quarter", "confidence": 0.60, "ai_generated": True},
    ])

# Later, extract and scan
metadata = akf.extract("quarterly-report.docx")
report = akf.scan("quarterly-report.docx")
print(f"Trust: {report.overall_trust:.2f}, AI claims: {report.ai_claim_count}")
```

### 6. TypeScript: Building with Normalization

```typescript
import { AKFBuilder, validate, effectiveTrust, fromJSON, toDescriptive } from 'akf';

// Build a unit
const unit = new AKFBuilder()
  .claim("API latency p99 < 200ms", 0.88, { src: "APM", tier: 2 })
  .claim("No critical CVEs", 0.95, { src: "Snyk", tier: 1, ver: true })
  .by("ci-pipeline")
  .label("internal")
  .build();

// Validate and check trust
const validation = validate(unit);
for (const claim of unit.claims) {
  const trust = effectiveTrust(claim);
  console.log(`"${claim.c}" -> ${trust.decision} (${trust.score.toFixed(2)})`);
}

// Accept descriptive input, normalize for processing
const loaded = fromJSON('{"version":"1.0","claims":[{"content":"test","confidence":0.8}]}');

// Convert to descriptive for display
const readable = toDescriptive(loaded);
console.log(JSON.stringify(readable, null, 2));
```

---

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/HMAKT99/AKF
- Spec: https://github.com/HMAKT99/AKF/blob/main/spec/akf-v1.0-spec.md
- JSON Schema: https://github.com/HMAKT99/AKF/blob/main/spec/akf-v1.0.schema.json
- Concise summary: https://akf.dev/llms.txt
